title: Transient (self-erasing) element — concept-level viability vs the standards-endorsed alternatives (prior-art survey)
summary: Grounds decision #1962 (is a self-erasing custom element the right idea AT ALL, given what the standards bodies endorse?) — case 1's facet of the framework-parity umbrella #1963. A transient (A-family) element (fui:blocks/transient/TransientElement.ts) builds a native element, moves its content onto it, then this.replaceWith(native) and vanishes. THE CRUX is WebKit standards-positions #97 (customized built-in elements, state: oppose; assignees rniwa/annevk): fetched verbatim, WebKit's reasoning is (1) form-associated custom elements solve the form-control use case better; (2) ElementInternals + ARIAMixin give autonomous elements good a11y; (3) customized built-ins can't access shadow/ElementInternals = second-class; (4) can't have good built-in a11y (logically different from the extended element); (5) progressive enhancement falls short — child elements are HTML's established fallback (canvas/picture); (6) is= syntax violates Priority of Constituencies; (7) only the HTML-parser case (td/template) is compelling, solved differently. KEY REFRAME: every WebKit objection is about customizing an EXISTING native element IN PLACE (is=); the transient element is a THIRD thing — it neither subclasses nor persists, it EMITS a real native element and deletes itself. So WebKit's constructive rec (persistent autonomous host + ElementInternals) argues for the persistent-B family and addresses OWNED near-native semantics on an element you KEEP — it does NOT deliver a true native element (real focus/activation/form/AT free), which is transient's only reason to exist. #97 is therefore CONSISTENT with the existing §7 cut (it recommends persistent exactly where §7 routes to B), not a refutation. ElementInternals grounding (frontierui: only 2 usages — AutoComplete.ts:52,386 form participation; declarativeComponent.ts:191-233 form+default-ARIA+custom-states): it grants form association + default role + custom states, NOT native behaviour (focus ring, keyboard activation, implicit submit, label click-through are HAND-WIRED), and applies only to your OWN autonomous element. Composition-cost grounding: behavioural depth composes to ZERO nodes via CustomAttribute (35+ behaviors, WeakMap out-of-band, CustomAttribute.ts:65-410) — transient buys nothing there; transient is a 1:1 node-ELIMINATION (author <we-button> → emit one <button>), not a stacking mechanism, so its honest justification narrows to ONE property: a real native element in the final DOM, authored declaratively, zero wrapper. Self-replacement is NOT spec-sanctioned (HTML warns vs tree-manip in reactions; connectedCallback fires >1x / isConnected false) and NO mainstream lib self-erases at runtime — but that yields a ROBUSTNESS RIDER (idempotent + isConnected-guarded + microtask-deferred), not an abandonment trigger — TransientElement ships two of the three legs (#replaced idempotency + queueMicrotask defer) but has NO isConnected guard, so that leg is a real one-line new requirement. The survey REFUTES the item's position D (scope transient by native-semantics PAYOFF): badge/tag/card are the CLEANEST transient cases (no state, no ref, no rename per #1961, zero wrapper) so moving them to persistent ADDS wrapper bloat to avoid a ~0 fragility cost — the correct discriminator is 'does a consumer need a persistent live ref / composite live-binding surface' (§7's existing cut: 13 A-family behaviour-free-leaf/single-native-control blocks vs 26 B-family reactive/grouped/ref-bound). is= (position C) is dead + statute-barred by the native-first single-substrate floor. Net: the concept-level challenge REAFFIRMS §7's persistent-ref cut, refutes D's discriminator, and adds a mandatory robustness rider. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-29-transient-self-erasure-concept-viability.md.
